other_observers: Elena Klaver, Mike Blatchley; Elena Klaver and I are the original finders of the bird.
description: A small black and white flycatcher. The bird was entirely black with white extending from the mid belly posteriorly past the legs. The bird was only slightly larger than the Barn Swallows with which it was fighting.Behaviors: The bird was flycatching and aggressively vying for perches with resident Barn Swallows. The bird would often perch on the bridge on Carter Ct. that crosses the NE trending irrigation canal. When we played a tape of Black Phoebe, it flew to the paved bike path we were on and perched in a nearby tree, and sang back.Call: Singing and calling. The songs heard were the a quick "phoebe" similar to Eastern Phoebe but shorter and given rapidly and a plaintive "beeee" slightly rising and then descending.Plumage: adult
